markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Prevent parallel agents from silently overwriting operator edits or each other in rich docs and graph claims. This MT belongs to ParallelSwarmStateRecovery. Make WP-009 safe for local/cloud swarm work, backend navigation, and repeated session recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Local and cloud swarm lanes must be observable, attributable, bounded, recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Every MT must leave enough receipts, state markers, and next-step context for session-limit recovery by a no-context model.
scope.expected_behavior: ParallelEditorAgentSafety leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ParallelEditorAgentSafety is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

commits.slice: MT-217 rich-document and graph-mutation claim safety

evidence.summary: MT-217 adds PostgreSQL-backed rich_document and graph_mutation claim scopes, explicit EditRichDocument/MutateGraph capabilities for local/operator/editor/system lanes, denial for validator/cloud/indexer lanes, and pre-side-effect validation that rejects malformed or mismatched editor mutation scopes before rows or EventLedger receipts can be written.

commands.result: RED before hardening: failed with expected invalid input containing rich_document.workspace_id, got success; GREEN after hardening: PASS 1/1

commands.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests editor -- --nocapture
commands.result: PASS; 3/3 MT-217 focused editor mutation claim tests passed
PASS; 19/19 parallel swarm state recovery PostgreSQL/EventLedger tests passed

evidence.summary: Reviewer initially failed the patch for malformed or misattributed rich_document/graph_mutation scopes persisting before reader rejection. Hardened with pre-insert scope validation and negative tests proving zero claim rows and zero parallel_swarm_claim receipts for malformed inputs. Re-review passed; residual risk is that the down migration fails closed if new-scope rows exist, and non-MT-217 legacy scope validation remains intentionally unchanged.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-217 (ParallelEditorAgentSafety) requires preventing parallel swarm agents from silently overwriting operator rich-document edits and graph-claim mutations, with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The diff adds real RichDocument/GraphMutation claim scopes, per-lane EditRichDocument/MutateGraph capabilities (granted to local/operator/editor/system, denied to validator/cloud/indexer), and pre-write scope validation that rejects malformed/mismatched scopes before any claim row or EventLedger receipt is written. Proof runs against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL with no SQLite/mock fallback.
findings: state_recovery.rs: adds ClaimScope::RichDocument{workspace_id,document_id} and ClaimScope::GraphMutation{workspace_id,graph_id} with kind() 'rich_document'/'graph_mutation' and composite scope_id format '{workspace}/{child}' (diff @ ClaimScope, scope_id, scope_from_parts).

state_recovery.rs:744-746: validate_claim_scope() is called in claim_work_surface BEFORE require_capability and BEFORE any DB write/reclaim, so malformed or mismatched-workspace scopes reject (StateRecoveryError::InvalidInput) prior to row insert or EventLedger receipt.

state_recovery.rs required_claim_capability(): RichDocument -> EditRichDocument, GraphMutation -> MutateGraph; capability grants give EditRichDocument/MutateGraph to Local/Operator/Editor/System lanes and withhold them from Validator/Cloud/Indexer lanes (capability vecs in AgentLaneIdentity).

migrations/0312_parallel_swarm_editor_agent_safety.sql: extends the real chk_agent_worktree_claims_scope_kind CHECK constraint to include 'rich_document','graph_mutation'; .down.sql restores the prior 3-value constraint. Operates on real knowledge_agent_worktree_claims table, no new table invented.

tests/parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests.rs: recovery_store() -> knowledge_pg() (knowledge_pg_support.rs:108) connects to Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (managed_postgres::ManagedPostgres::ensure_running) on an isolated schema with the full migration chain; header explicitly states NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback.

Test editor_document_and_graph_claims_serialize_parallel_mutations: proves second concurrent claim on same document/graph returns ClaimStatus::Held naming the active holder, different document proceeds Active, release frees the claim, and persisted scope_ids are queried from the real pool.

Test non_editor_lanes_cannot_claim_editor_mutation_scopes: validator/cloud/indexer denied with InvalidInput naming EditRichDocument/MutateGraph; asserts COUNT(*)==0 claim rows and 0 parallel_swarm_claim EventLedger receipts for the denied workspace; editor lane allowed claim succeeds.

Test malformed_editor_mutation_scopes_do_not_persist_claims_or_receipts: 6 malformed cases (empty/slash segments, mismatched workspace) all rejected; asserts 0 persisted claim rows and 0 EventLedger receipts against the real pool. This is the RED->GREEN test cited in lifecycle.evidence.
No deferred-live escapes: grep of state_recovery.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/*Unavailable/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-' returned no hits on the required behavior path.

Self-cert: lifecycle.claimed_by=null, completed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260612, status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION (not a self-signed COMPLETED); independent review proceeds. Aside: packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194 (expected paperwork drift, non-blocking).
evidence_checked: wtc-kernel-009 git show 91efe116 (state_recovery.rs, 0312 up/down migrations, parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests.rs)

src/backend/handshake_core/src/swarm_orchestration/state_recovery.rs (claim_work_surface, validate_claim_scope, required_claim_capability, capability grants, append_event_tx, INSERT INTO knowledge_agent_worktree_claims)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
